title: Perception
description: DRAFT v0 — L1 perception: literal observation of ONE ad image. Geometry+text (axes 1-2) + scene+look (axes 3-4), observe-only. Records WHAT IS THERE / WHERE / HOW IT LOOKS — never what it MEANS. NO impression-naming (premium/honest/hero) and NO register/mood: that is the downstream visual-analyst (ring 2). Enum BUCKETING of scene/look is also downstream — here, literal facts only. Supersedes ocr-extraction.schema.json.

medium.description: Axis 3 GATE — the rendering medium of the image. Most-observable, always determinable. GATES the photo-only fields: scene.shot_scale/angle and look.lighting/finish are meaningful ONLY when medium=photo (or the photo part of a composite); OMIT them otherwise (omission is the signal, not 'other'). A flat promo / illustration has no camera and no light source.

text_confidence.description: OCR-faithful per-element read confidence (1=crisp/certain, low=blurry/overlapping/decorative). Lets a downstream consumer trust this text or escalate WITHOUT the image. The schema is the image substitute.

scene.description: Axis 3 — what is depicted, as LITERAL observation. subjects = presence facts; depicted = literal scene sentence; shot_scale/angle = literal framing geometry (photo-only). The in_use-vs-standalone distinction is NOT bucketed here — it is DERIVED downstream by visual-analyst from the presence of product + human_part. No setting/register bucketing here.

note.description: literal detail only (e.g. 'hand holding the box'); never an impression or staged/real judgement

depicted.description: LITERAL scene description any second viewer would transcribe identically, e.g. 'a hand pouring liquid from a bottle into a glass on a wooden table'. NEVER the impression (no premium/honest/playful) and NEVER a real/fake/staged judgement (a chat-app screenshot is 'a messaging-app chat screenshot', never 'a fake chat') — those are downstream.

space.description: PHOTO-ONLY literal background-space cue that disambiguates studio vs real environment downstream — added after the two-worker pilot found 'plain wall' was ambiguous between studio and room. seamless_backdrop = no corner/floor visible (studio sweep); real_room = a corner/floor/furniture is visible; outdoor = sky/ground/foliage; surface_top = shot down onto a tabletop; none = no spatial scene (graphic).

look.description: Axis 4 — literal light/finish facts, observable by any viewer. NOT register/mood (premium/honest is named downstream by visual-analyst).

lighting.description: PHOTO-ONLY (medium=photo). Omit when there is no light source (illustration/flat_graphic).

brightness.description: Applies to any medium (perceivable on graphics and photos alike).

finish.description: PRODUCT-SURFACE fact — the finish of the dominant product surface if one is present. Omit on lifestyle/illustration/graphic frames with no dominant product surface.

look_desc.description: free-text LITERAL light/surface description if no enum fits — same observe-only guardrail: no impression, no register, no real/fake judgement

not_present.description: Absence as signal — salient things a viewer would expect that are NOT shown.

observation_confidence.description: Per-axis confidence in the observation. Travels downstream so a low-confidence read is never silently promoted to fact.
